notes: Push day. Right shoulder felt weak/some pain so swapped bench press for chest press machine — all racks were taken anyway. Good call avoiding bench. Chest press topped out at 185x4. Incline DB at 55 was hard but consistent — 3x10 then 9 on last set. Push press still can't break past 45/side, 50s wouldn't even go overhead. Pec deck: accidentally discovered 5lb was on, jumped to 115x9 to failure — nice! Cable lat raises at 50 felt easy, jumped to 65 briefly. Tricep extension progressed to 65x12. Added straight arm pulldown back — 105x12 still easy, needs more weight. Did a 1hr zone 1 hilly run on Sunday.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back. Sunday: 1hr zone 1 hilly run.

exercises.notes: SWAPPED from bench press — right shoulder felt weak/pain, plus all racks taken. First set struggled at 175, then adjusted the start position (was too far behind back). 175x8 felt slightly better. Tried 185 — barely got 4. 180x3 and called it. Smart to avoid bench with the shoulder issue.

55x10 hard from set 1. Got 3x10 but each set was a fight, especially the last. Fourth set only got 9. Asked 'does hard but did it mean it was easy?' — no, consistent at 55x10 with degradation = right weight. Stay here until 4x10 is clean.

50/side absolutely would not go overhead. Dropped to 45 — got 5/6/5. W3 was 6/6/5 at 45. Slightly worse — shoulder fatigue from chest press + incline probably a factor. Not getting worse at this, just doing it later in the session with a sore shoulder.
15x15 for 4 sets, probably too easy. Drop set to 10 at end. Hard to find 20lb DBs at the gym. Next time: try 20s if available, or bump to 5 sets at 15.
105x12 twice — realized a 5lb add-on plate was on from W3. So W3's '100' was actually 105. Tried 115 — hit 9 to failure. Real progression: W3 105x12 → W4 115x9. 115 is the new challenge weight.
50x12 felt easy with good form — big jump from W3's 30. Tried 65 — got 6 then dropped to 55 for 4 more. 50 is the working weight, 65 is a stretch goal.
55x12 felt way too easy. Jumped to 65 — really hard at 10. Third set got 12 at 65 but form started breaking at the end. 65 is the new working weight. Keep hands apart.
Added back — was in plan but got missed in the workout file. Pyramided up: 70, 90, 105 — all x12 and all felt too easy. Need to go heavier next time, start at 105 or 110+.

notes: KB Explore — GTC light day. Followed the workout precisely. OTD flow at 25lb was the highlight — did 3 rounds per side, and ran the entire flow 3 times. Felt pretty fluid by the last set. Skill work at 25lb was good but shoulders were dead by that point from the flow's bent presses. C&P at 50lb still ridiculously hard but completed all 6x2 reps, losing form at the end.

exercises.notes: OTD flow (Madeline Conti). 3 rounds per side, entire flow done 3 times (way more than prescribed). Felt fluid by the last set. 25lb is the right weight for flow work — heavy enough to demand control, light enough to chain moves.
Isolated skill work after the flow. 25lb was fine but shoulders dead from all the bent presses in the flow. Good form though.
Solid at 25.
Light and easy at 25 — intentionally light for GTC day.
Still ridiculously hard. Completed all 6x2 reps but really hard at the end and losing form. The 50lb C&P after all those flow bent presses is a lot of overhead work.
Finished strong.

notes: Tough day — 30C heat wave in March, completely wiped from GTC demos (10hrs on feet yesterday). Pull-ups down to 33 total (9/8/8/4+4) vs W3's 32 — technically beat it but felt way worse. DB rows had good form, especially on later sets. Lat pulldown at 150 was weirdly easy vs pull-ups being hard — 150x12 on sets 2 and 3. Cable row solid at 130x12. Bicep curl and preacher curl completely died — arms were toast by the end. Heat + GTC fatigue = worst feeling session but still got through everything.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

exercises.notes: 33 total (9/8/8/8 counting the split). Barely beats W3's 32. Tired from GTC and heat. Last set had to split 4+4. Form dropping after rest on set 3 — unusual.
Did all prescribed sets. Good form on the later heavy sets — not rushing and not super slow. No grip problems today (unlike W3 where hands were dead).

145 wasn't available so did 150. Weirdly easy despite pull-ups being hard — different movement pattern. 150x12 on sets 2-3 with good form til end. Forgot to bump to 155 on set 3. 150 is now the working weight.
130x12 across all 3 sets. Second set was hard, rested extra before third. W3: 120 was honest, 140 was too much. 130 is the right working weight — confirmed.
50x20 for all three. Form breaking around rep 17 on sets 2-3. Same pattern as W3 — 50 is at the edge where volume degrades form.
3x12 then died at 9 on set 4. W3 was 3x12 + 10. Slightly worse — fatigue and heat. Was supposed to be 3 sets? Did 4 anyway. Arms completely toast.
All 3x15 completed. Form dropping around rep 12 on set 2. Needed extra rest before set 3 to finish clean. Consistent with W3.

Completely dead! Got nowhere close to W3's numbers. Dropped to 10/side and could only manage 8. Last set was painfully slow. Arms were already toast from cable curls. Don't judge this — biceps were fully cooked before getting here.

notes: Legs day with well-rested legs — rest day yesterday (GTC), no KB before. Squat hit 70/side x8 deep for the first time! Fresh legs theory confirmed. RDL skipped (not mentioned). Leg press hips were tight from standing all week — first set couldn't get full ROM but second set opened up. Calf raises at 6 plates/side still getting 3x20 somehow — calves are absurd. Leg curl jumped to 110x12. Lunges at 40lb DBs were death as usual. Skipped KB Saturday and weekend — needed sleep and recovery from GTC week.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

exercises.notes: Fresh legs = huge difference. 55x8 warmup then straight into 65x8 deep for 3 sets — no struggling to find depth like W3. Then hit 70/side x8 DEEP — new PR weight! Still hard at the end but had room. Rest day before squats is the move. W3: needed 2 warmup sets and struggled at 65. W4: 65 felt routine, 70 was achievable.

sets: 4 plates + 25/side x12 (hard, couldn't get full ROM)
exercises.notes: Hips tight from standing all week at GTC — first set couldn't get as deep as W3's knee-to-chest. Second set way better, full ROM. Probably too easy weight-wise — should go up next time. Hip tightness was the limiter, not strength.

Bumped from 5 to 6 plates/side — really hard! Doing them slower too. Still getting to 20 all 3 sets. What is wrong with these calves. Easiest exercise of the day despite the weight jump. Bump again next time?
90 too easy now. Jumped to 110 — hard but completed all 12 with full ROM and slow tempo. Did all three sets. W3: 105 was the challenge weight, now 110. Progression: W2 50 → W3 90/105 → W4 110.
First set felt impossible but went as deep as possible and completed it. Only logged one set but completed the prescribed work. Absolute death as always — lunges are the finisher that finishes you.
